---
title: "Pennate"
subtitle: "Catch-all for elongate, bilaterally symmetric pennate diatoms that can't be confidently assigned to a more specific genus."
categories: [Diatoms]
---

<!-- GENERATED by scripts/build_pages.py — do not edit by hand. Edit meta.yml and re-run the script. -->

**Group:** Diatoms  ·  **Optics:** high-mag, low-mag

## Defining characteristics

Pennate diatoms are elongate and bilaterally (mirror) symmetric — boat-, rod-, needle-, or wedge-shaped in valve view — with striae arranged relative to a central long axis, and often a raphe (a lengthwise slit). Use this class for pennate cells that are clearly not centric but cannot be keyed with confidence to one of the named pennate genera in this guide. Key features: elongate bilateral outline, lengthwise (not radial) stria pattern, no setae.

## Distinguishing from similar classes

| Similar class | How to tell them apart |
|---|---|
| **pseudo-nitzschia** | Label Pseudo-nitzschia when narrow needle-like cells form stepped, end-overlapping colonies. Use Pennate only when the genus is unclear. |
| **thalassionema** | Thalassionema forms zigzag or stellate colonies of blunt bars joined at the corners — label that genus when the colony form is evident. |
| **chaetoceros** | Chaetoceros and other centric diatoms are radially symmetric (often with setae); Pennate cells are bilaterally symmetric with no setae. |

## Ideal images

_No images yet — add files to this folder._

## Challenging images

_No images yet — add files to this folder._

## References

- [UCSC Ocean Data Center — Phytoplankton Gallery](http://oceandatacenter.ucsc.edu/PhytoGallery/)
